name: circuit_cost_scaling
description: Circuit-cost scaling of CH vs QLBM/QALB across the q-ladder -- the QALB story the accuracy figures miss. QLBM's defining property: cost is GRID-INDEPENDENT (flat ~119,440 CX on a fixed 9 qubits at every q), the price of a constant-size circuit. CH is the opposite: cheap at low q but cost grows with the grid. The two methods have mirror-image cost scaling, matching their mirror-image accuracy/stability. Costs read from q8020_analysis_0.json (cx, circuit_depth, n_qubits); transpiled at opt-level 1, all-to-all (real-device CX higher after routing).

global._source_cases: mutual_q{4,6,7,8}_nu050 (cx/depth/n_qubits are physics-independent for QLBM and grid-driven for CH; the nu050 series is the representative dial-up)

_takeaways: QLBM CX is flat at 119,440 and qubits flat at 9 across q4-q8: grid handled by measure_reprepare, not more gates.
CH CX grows with the grid (300 -> 33,120) and qubits 10 -> 20: grid-dependent circuit.

Crossover: CH is far cheaper through q7-q8 in this range, but its cost trend is rising while QLBM is constant -- extrapolated, QLBM's flat cost wins at high enough q. The hardware-relevant point today (q4: CH ~300 CX vs QLBM 119k) favors CH; QLBM is sim-only at any q (119k CX -> ~0 fidelity).
Note q8 CH n_qubits (17) < q7 (20): the CH ancilla/bond-dim layout is not strictly monotonic in q; cx still rises.
